query: My XGBoost classifier has 96% training accuracy and 78% test accuracy on a tabular fraud dataset. Audit it for overfit using the deep-learning overfit workflow.
expected_behavior: Identifies XGBoost as classical / tree-ensemble ML, not a deep network, and does NOT apply the deep-learning-specific diagnostics (per-epoch loss curves, weight-norm trajectory, effective-rank) as the primary workflow
Recommends classical-ML evaluation approaches instead — at minimum names k-fold cross-validation, train-vs-test gap inspection, or the XGBoost-native early_stopping_rounds parameter
Suggests handing off to a more appropriate skill (e.g., evaluating-binary-classifiers or evaluating-multiclass-classifiers) rather than forcing the deep-learning overfit workflow onto a tree ensemble
